excerpt: Beethoven and the Heroic ; Part 2: Leonore Notes by Fred Haight https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_HNC4jdDQ0M&feature=emb_logo Part 2: Leonore No-one ever portrayed a woman more heroically then Beethoven. His only opera, Fidelio, is about a woman named Leonore, who courageously disguises herself as a boy, goes into prison, risking her life, in order to rescue her husband, Florestan, who is a political prisoner. The opera was inspired by the real-life story of Adrienne LaFayette, who went into an Austrian prison, to free her husband, The Marquis de LaFayette, a hero of the American Revolution.
